sections.title: Course Overview
sections.text_content:
# Welcome to JAMB UTME Prep 2026!

## What Makes This Course Different?

✅ **Comprehensive Coverage**: Every topic in all 4 subjects
✅ **20+ Mock Exams**: Practice in real exam conditions
✅ **Performance Tracking**: See your improvement week by week
✅ **Flexible Learning**: Study at your own pace
✅ **Expert Strategies**: Learn shortcuts and time-saving techniques

## Course Structure:

**Weeks 1-2:** English Language mastery
**Weeks 3-4:** Mathematics intensive
**Weeks 5-6:** Science subjects (Physics & Chemistry)
**Week 7:** Speed & accuracy training
**Week 8:** Final revision & mock exams

## How to Succeed:

1. **Follow the schedule**: 2 hours daily minimum
2. **Take all mock exams**: They reveal your weak areas
3. **Review mistakes**: Learn from every error
4. **Practice timed drills**: Build speed
5. **Stay consistent**: Daily practice beats cramming

resources.title: 8-Week Study Timetable
resources.description: Daily breakdown of what to study

lessons.title: Test-Taking Strategies: The Fundamentals
lessons.description: Master the strategies that boost scores by 30-50 points
learning_objectives: Manage your time (1 minute per question)

sections.title: Strategy 1: The Elimination Method
sections.text_content:
# The Elimination Strategy

## When to Use:
- You don't know the answer immediately
- Multiple options seem possible
- Time is running out

## How It Works:

**Step 1:** Remove obviously wrong answers
- Too extreme (always, never, all, none)
- Contradicts the question
- Contains unfamiliar terms

**Step 2:** Analyze remaining options
- Look for similarities
- Apply logic
- Make educated guess

## Example:

**Question:** Which of the following is NOT a programming language?

A) Python
B) JavaScript
C) Microsoft
D) Java

**Process:**
- A, B, D are known programming languages ✓
- C is a company, not a language ✗

**Answer:** C

⏱️ **Time Saved:** 30 seconds vs. overthinking

## Practice This Strategy:
You'll use this in 40-50% of questions!

sections.title: Video: Strategies in Action
sections.description: Watch me solve 10 questions using elimination

quiz_questions.text: In a class of 40 students, 25 play football and 20 play basketball. If 10 play both games, how many play neither?
quiz_questions.explanation: Use set theory: Total = (Football only) + (Basketball only) + (Both) + (Neither). 40 = (25-10) + (20-10) + 10 + x. Solving: x = 5
Solve: 2x = 11 - 3 = 8, so x = 4. Should take 20 seconds.
Benevolent means kind and generous. Eliminate harsh (opposite), indifferent (neutral), hostile (opposite). Choose kind.

lessons.title: Diagnostic Mock Exam - Full JAMB Simulation
lessons.description: Take your first full mock exam to establish baseline score

sections.title: Before You Begin
sections.text_content:
# Diagnostic Mock Exam Instructions

## Important Rules:

1. ✅ **Find a quiet place** - No distractions for 3 hours
2. ⏱️ **Set 180 minutes timer** - Real exam timing
3. 🚫 **No breaks** - Complete in one sitting
4. 📱 **No external help** - No Google, no notes
5. 📝 **Answer all questions** - No penalty for wrong answers

## Exam Structure:

- **English Language:** 60 questions
- **Mathematics:** 60 questions
- **Physics:** 40 questions
- **Chemistry:** 40 questions

**Total:** 180 questions in 180 minutes (1 question/minute)

## After the Exam:

You'll receive:
- Overall score
- Score per subject
- Weak topics identified
- Personalized study recommendations

## Ready?

When you click 'Start Exam', the timer begins. Good luck! 🍀

quiz_questions.text: [ENGLISH] Choose the option nearest in meaning to: 'The manager VINDICATED his decision.'
quiz_questions.explanation: Vindicated means justified or defended. The manager defended/justified his decision.
quiz_questions.subject: english

quiz_questions.text: [MATHEMATICS] If 3x - 2 = 10, find the value of x
quiz_questions.explanation: Add 2 to both sides: 3x = 12. Divide by 3: x = 4
quiz_questions.subject: mathematics

quiz_questions.text: [PHYSICS] The SI unit of force is:
quiz_questions.explanation: Force = mass × acceleration. Unit: kg × m/s² = Newton (N)
quiz_questions.subject: physics

quiz_questions.text: [CHEMISTRY] The atomic number of an element is determined by the number of:
quiz_questions.explanation: Atomic number = number of protons in the nucleus
quiz_questions.subject: chemistry

sections.title: 📊 Your Diagnostic Results
sections.text_content:
# Diagnostic Exam Complete!

## What Happens Next?

After submitting, you'll see:

1. **Overall Score** - Your total out of 400
2. **Subject Breakdown** - Score per subject
3. **Topic Analysis** - Which topics need work
4. **Percentile Rank** - How you compare to others
5. **Study Recommendations** - Personalized plan

## Interpreting Your Score:

- **300-400:** Excellent! Focus on maintaining consistency
- **250-299:** Good foundation, target weak areas
- **200-249:** Solid start, significant room for improvement
- **150-199:** Need intensive study, follow plan closely
- **Below 150:** Don't worry! Average improvement is 45+ points

## Remember:

This is your BASELINE. Most students score 30-50 points lower on their first attempt compared to the real exam. The course will help you improve!

**Next Step:** Review your results and start Week 2 lessons.

modules.title: Week 2-3: English Language Mastery
modules.description: Master all aspects of JAMB English - comprehension, lexis, structure, and oral forms

lessons.title: Comprehension Strategies
lessons.description: Learn to answer comprehension questions quickly and accurately

sections.title: The 3-Step Method
sections.text_content:
# Comprehension: The 3-Step Method

## Step 1: Skim First (30 seconds)
- Read only the first and last paragraph
- Identify the main idea
- Don't read the whole passage yet!

## Step 2: Read Questions (1 minute)
- Read all questions carefully
- Underline key words in questions
- Know what to look for

## Step 3: Targeted Reading (4 minutes)
- Now read the passage
- When you find an answer, mark it immediately
- Don't re-read unnecessarily

**Total Time:** ~5-6 minutes for 5 questions = Success!

Vs.

**Wrong Method:** Read passage, then questions, then re-read = 10 minutes wasted ❌

sections.title: Video: Method in Action
sections.description: Watch me solve a full comprehension passage in 6 minutes

quiz_questions.text: Read the passage and answer: What is the main idea?
quiz_questions.explanation: The passage focuses on climate change effects. Key phrases: 'global warming', 'rising temperatures', 'environmental impact'

options.text: Climate change and its environmental effects
